title: The Ultimate Spider-Man Run Is Reinvigorating The Web-Slinger
excerpt: Jonathan Hickman and Marco Checchetto's new Ultimate Spider-Man is exactly what fans have been begging for: Peter Parker as an adult, married to MJ, with kids.

children.text: For years, Spider-Man fans have had the same complaint about the mainline Amazing Spider-Man book: Peter Parker is stuck in perpetual adolescence. He can never hold a job, his relationships always fail, and the Parker Luck always resets his life back to zero.

content.quote: This isn't just a reboot. It's a fundamental reimagining of what Peter Parker's life could have been if he wasn't bitten until adulthood.

children.text: By skipping the teenage years entirely and having Peter get bitten by the radioactive spider as a 35-year-old married father of two, the series instantly bypasses decades of tired tropes.
A masterclass in comic book pacing. Marco Checchetto's art combined with Hickman's methodical world-building makes this the best Spider-Man book on the shelves today.
A Different Kind of Uncle Ben

One of the most striking changes in this universe is that Uncle Ben is alive. Not only is he alive, but he's a veteran journalist working alongside J. Jonah Jameson. Their dynamic as the grizzled newsmen trying to uncover the truth about their world's shadowy rulers adds a thrilling investigative layer to the story.

title: Venom War: The Symbiote Event We Deserve
excerpt: Eddie Brock and Dylan Brock are going to war. Here's everything you need to know about Marvel's next massive symbiote crossover.

children.text: The symbiote corner of the Marvel Universe has been expanding rapidly ever since Donny Cates' legendary run. Now, Al Ewing is taking it to the next level with
Eddie Brock and his son Dylan both have legitimate claims to the Venom symbiote. This event pits father against son, with the entire Marvel Universe caught in the crossfire.

We've seen symbiote events before (Maximum Carnage, Absolute Carnage, King in Black), but this one feels deeply personal. It's not just about a world-ending threat; it's a family dispute that happens to involve shape-shifting alien goo and massive collateral damage.
